summary.intent: Improve the browser URL bar UX by showing error messages to the user when URL navigation fails, instead of silently doing nothing.
summary.outcome: Centralized error handling in the `browserAction` helper so all browser API calls that return `success: false` automatically surface errors to the user, and removed the now-redundant per-call check in `navigateSession`.

repo: The browser manager API returns errors as HTTP 200 with `success: false` in the body, not as non-2xx status codes — callers must check the response body explicitly.
Prior to this fix, `browserAction` only threw on non-2xx HTTP, so `success: false` responses were silently swallowed.

code.finding: The `browserAction` helper is the centralized wrapper for all browser manager API calls. Adding `success` checking here covers all actions without touching each call site.

workflow: JS is linted before committing (`Lint JS` step in the workflow).
Centralizing error handling in a shared helper reduces duplication and ensures consistent behavior across all call sites.

friction: The silent failure mode (200 OK with `success: false`) was non-obvious and caused a hard-to-diagnose 'Waiting for first frame' bug — the URL bar appeared to work but navigation never happened.
